name: AP Atlas Explorer Seed
description: A relationship map for Asymmetric Precision v2. Relationships are first-class citizens.

nodes.label: Asymmetric Precision
nodes.summary: The public home of Applied Systems Thinking and the center of gravity for AP books, essays, frameworks, field notes, laboratories, and visual language.

nodes.label: The AP Oath
nodes.summary: The front-door invitation into the AP way of thinking: question assumptions, follow evidence, understand before judging, and leave systems better than you found them.

nodes.label: AP Philosophy
nodes.summary: The core explanation of why AP exists, what it believes, and how it approaches complex systems.

nodes.label: See the System
nodes.summary: Understand the whole before optimizing the parts. Every action exists inside a larger pattern of relationships, incentives, and constraints.

nodes.label: Build Better Mental Models
nodes.summary: Complex problems rarely require more information. They require better ways of thinking.

nodes.label: Evidence Over Assumption
nodes.summary: Good decisions come from understanding reality as it is. The system is usually telling the truth.

nodes.label: Design for Outcomes
nodes.summary: Activities matter only insofar as they create meaningful outcomes. Motion is not progress.

nodes.label: Practical Over Theoretical
nodes.summary: Frameworks should improve real organizations, not just explain them. Ideas should survive contact with reality.

nodes.label: Relationships First
nodes.summary: Objects matter, but relationships create behavior. The Atlas treats connections as first-class citizens.

nodes.label: Intentional All the Way Down
nodes.summary: Every decision communicates. Every name should teach. Nothing is just implementation.

nodes.label: Hidden Systems
nodes.summary: Invisible relationships, incentives, constraints, trust, authority, culture, and feedback that shape outcomes before failure becomes visible.

nodes.label: Tactical Compassion
nodes.summary: Precision with humanity; compassion with discipline. The strongest systems balance both.

nodes.label: Understanding
nodes.summary: The primary AP success measure: did someone leave seeing the system more clearly than when they arrived?

nodes.label: Hidden Systems Visual Language
nodes.summary: A visual language for making hidden relationships, telemetry, constraints, uncertainty, and system states visible.

nodes.label: Atlas
nodes.summary: The connected map of AP ideas, domains, principles, frameworks, essays, books, observations, and laboratories.

nodes.label: Books
nodes.summary: The long-form AP curriculum for complex systems, leadership, trust, control, and operational architecture.

nodes.label: Essays
nodes.summary: Short, sharp explorations of AP ideas that help people name, frame, and understand systems.

nodes.label: Frameworks
nodes.summary: Reusable mental models, maps, and decision structures for real systems under pressure.

nodes.label: Laboratories
nodes.summary: Practical implementations where AP ideas become tools, software, games, and experiments.

nodes.label: Field Notes
nodes.summary: Operational observations from systems under pressure.

nodes.label: Zero Trust
nodes.summary: A cybersecurity application space where AP thinking turns activity into outcomes, policy into architecture, and trust into evidence.

nodes.label: RMF as Risk Communication
nodes.summary: RMF is most valuable when treated as governance and risk communication, not as a substitute for security engineering.

nodes.label: KGB Studio
nodes.summary: A software laboratory for turning AP system clarity into practical creative tooling.

nodes.label: KGB Arcade
nodes.summary: A game laboratory where systems, feedback, constraints, and learning become playable.
